Directions

This Demonstration shows the temperature-time behavior for an exothermic, liquid-phase, first-order reaction in a batch reactor that is scaled up geometrically. A cooling jacket around the reactor transfers heat to a heat transfer fluid. Use sliders to vary the temperature of the heat transfer fluid and the reactor height and diameter (diameter = height). As the height and diameter increase, the (surface area for heat transfer)/volume decreases, and thus the maximum temperature increases. This shows why geometric scale-up is not appropriate for this system. Check "adiabatic" to view the temperature-time behavior for an adiabatic reactor; this behavior is independent of the reactor size.
